note: jquery_ui_controlgroup is a passive asset/library provider: it ships only an .info.yml (no *.libraries.yml, PHP, config entities, settings, permissions, plugins, or services), and the base jquery_ui module registers the `jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup` library on its behalf. There is therefore NO execution surface to build and verify, so HARD (mode: execution) cases are intentionally OMITTED. One MEDIUM (mode: introspection) case is included: it needs no setup/cleanup because there is no config to seed — instead the agent must query the LIVE library registry (a real static live fact) and report the actual JS/CSS asset filenames, which proves it can inspect running-site state rather than recite docs. Remaining cases are EASY (recipe), response-graded, grounded in data.json, usage.md, and agent/*.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what does the contrib `jquery_ui_controlgroup` module actually provide, and what is the id of the asset library it exposes?
evals.expected_output: It re-provides the jQuery UI Controlgroup widget as a Drupal asset library after jQuery UI was removed from core. The library id is jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup. It is a thin dependency-provider, not a feature module.

evals.prompt: Why does the jquery_ui_controlgroup module exist as a separate contrib project, and what does it depend on?
evals.expected_output: Drupal core removed the bundled jQuery UI libraries and split each widget into its own contrib project. jquery_ui_controlgroup re-exposes the Controlgroup widget and depends on the base jquery_ui module (>=8.x-1.7), which ships the actual jQuery UI assets. It is a compatibility bridge for legacy code.

evals.prompt: In my own module's *.libraries.yml, how do I make my JS library depend on jQuery UI Controlgroup so its assets load? Show the YAML.
evals.expected_output: Under your library's `dependencies:` list `- jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup`. Attaching that library also pulls in core/jquery, jquery_ui/widget, and jquery_ui/internal.widget-css automatically.

evals.prompt: Where is the configuration/settings page for the jquery_ui_controlgroup module, and what permissions does it add?
evals.expected_output: There is none: jquery_ui_controlgroup has no configuration UI (its configure route is null), no permissions, no plugins and no services. You just install it and attach the jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup library where you need it.

evals.prompt: On this live Drupal site, is `jquery_ui_controlgroup/controlgroup` a registered asset library? Inspect the running site's library registry (do not guess) and report the JS and CSS asset files it declares.
evals.expected_output: Yes. Querying the library.discovery service (e.g. `drush php:eval` on \Drupal::service('library.discovery')->getLibrariesByExtension('jquery_ui_controlgroup')) shows the `controlgroup` library (jQuery UI 1.13.2). Its JS asset is controlgroup-min.js and its CSS asset is controlgroup.css, both served from the base jquery_ui module at modules/contrib/jquery_ui/assets/vendor/jquery.ui/ (ui/widgets/controlgroup-min.js and themes/base/controlgroup.css).
